# --------------------------------------------------------------
# test_title_case.py
# --------------------------------------------------------------
# Unit tests for the ``title_case`` function.
# --------------------------------------------------------------

import pytest

# --------------------------------------------------------------
# The function under test (normally you would import it from your module)
# --------------------------------------------------------------
def title_case(s: str) -> str:
    """Capitalise the first letter of each word and lower‑case the rest."""
    return " ".join(word.capitalize() for word in s.split())


# --------------------------------------------------------------
# 1️⃣  Happy‑path tests – normal inputs that should produce a correctly
#     title‑cased string.
# --------------------------------------------------------------
class TestTitleCaseHappyPath:
    """Tests for typical, well‑formed inputs."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "input_str, expected",
        [
            # Basic lower‑case
            ("hello world", "Hello World"),
            # All upper‑case
            ("HELLO WORLD", "Hello World"),
            # Mixed case
            ("HeLLo WoRLD", "Hello World"),
            # More than two words
            ("python is awesome", "Python Is Awesome"),
            # Unicode letters with diacritics
            ("café", "Café"),
            ("ÑOÑO", "Ñoño"),
            ("über", "Über"),
